10160595908183787.subject:
Your video, A tribute to Dravid

If cricket is a gentleman's game then that gentleman has to be Rahul Dravid. Can't think of another person who has carried himself with the same poise, elegance, dedication and commitment like he has done. But did cricket serve him what he deserved - not really. The biggest and perhaps the only major success (as a team) of his life came on the last day of his association with cricket - both life and cricket at times can be really testing and cruel and the it's best students are put through the most stringent tests. And when life tests us let's a be dravid and not a Gandhi (dig intended).

Thank you Mr Dravid for showing us that life can he lived like this too that game can be played like this too both as a player and as a coach and the more committed you are more it will test you but there is redemption at the end sometimes at the very end.

If I had a son i would have liked him to become like rahul Dravid in whatever vocation/profession he chose.

Quoting shakespeare

His life was gentle; and the elements
So mixed in him, that Nature might stand up
And say to all the world, THIS WAS A MAN!

That man to me/us is Rahul Dravid, has new copyright updates to review.

messages.subject: Thanks for your feedback
messages.message: Case number: 10157886584838787 Thanks for your report â you did the right thing by letting us know about this. The group you reported was reviewed, and although it doesn't go against one of our specific Community Standards, we understand that the group or something shared in it may still be offensive to you. We want to help you avoid things you don't want to see on Facebook. If you think we should review something specific in this or another group, you can report that exact content (e.g. photo) instead of the entire group.  From the list above, you can also hide, unfollow or leave  directly. We recommend visiting the Help Centre to learn more about how to control what you see in your News Feed. If you find that a person, group or Page consistently posts things you don't want to see, you may want to limit how often you see their posts or remove them from your Facebook experience. We know that these options may not apply to every situation, so please let us know if you see something else you think that we should take a look at.
